summary: I was born and lived in Mexico City for 40 years. It’s a place full of pollution and traffic, which is something I wanted to leave behind for my family. Most of my work family was also born there, and although life was challenging, it shaped who I am. Leaving Mexico City was part of my journey to find a better life for my children.

Mexico is my home country, and despite its challenges like insecurity, a deficient education system, and low salaries, it has positively influenced me and my family. We are honest and hardworking people, and I carry that with me wherever I go. However, the harsh situation there pushed me to seek better opportunities abroad for my children’s future.

Anchorage, Alaska is where my life took a new turn after I became a widow. It’s been 15 months since we moved here, and adapting to the cold winters and language has been tough. This city is where I met my current husband through my uncle, and it’s where my family has grown closer despite missing our relatives back home. Living here has brought us closer to nature and away from the chaos of Mexico City.

Living in the United States has been a dream come true for me since childhood. My family and I visited the US for the first time as a family, exploring places like Disneyland, SeaWorld, and Legoland, and visiting cousins in San Diego and Los Angeles. Now that we live here, we appreciate the opportunities, security, and natural surroundings that the country offers. It’s a place where I can work hard and provide a better future for my children.

Our first family visit to the United States included a memorable trip to Disneyland. It was one of the best experiences we had together, filled with joy and excitement for my children. Visiting Disneyland along with other attractions made that trip very special and unforgettable for all of us.

SeaWorld was part of our first family trip to the United States, and it was an amazing experience for my kids. They loved seeing the marine life and enjoying the shows, which added to the fun of our visit. It’s one of the places that made our trip to the US so special and memorable.

During our first visit to the United States, we also went to Legoland, which was a highlight for my children. They were fascinated by the creativity and fun of the park, and it brought us a lot of happiness as a family. That trip, including Legoland, helped us bond and create lasting memories.

San Diego is where some of my cousins live, and we had the chance to visit them during our first trip to the United States. It was wonderful to reconnect with family there and share special moments together. Visiting San Diego added a personal touch to our trip and helped my children feel closer to their relatives.

Los Angeles was another important stop during our first family visit to the United States. We visited cousins there, which made the trip more meaningful and helped us maintain family ties. The experience in Los Angeles, along with other places, made our visit truly memorable and joyful.
